input.instructions: Given a graph of colors and their adjacency requirements, design an algorithm to place these colors on a 50x50 grid. Each color must occupy at least one cell, all cells of the same color must be connected, and two colors can only be adjacent if they have an edge in the graph. The goal is to minimize the number of cells used by colors to maximize the number of 0 cells.

variables.selected_prompt_text:
Solve the question directly.
Keep the entire JSON compact.
Keep decision_notes extremely short. Prefer 3 to 8 words.
Do not narrate your reasoning outside the response field.
In the response field, use exactly two short lines encoded with \n:
FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
Keep the justification to 12 words or fewer.
Use plain text only inside the response field. Do not use markdown formatting, code fences, or math-style backslashes such as \equiv.
Do not add any extra text before or after the JSON object.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",
  "schema_version": "v1",
  "orchestration_decision": "execute_locally",
  "decision_notes": "optional string",
  "response": "FINAL_ANSWER: <answer>\nJUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>",
  "result_type": "summary"
}

Question:
Given a graph of colors and their adjacency requirements, design an algorithm to place these colors on a 50x50 grid. Each color must occupy at least one cell, all cells of the same color must be connected, and two colors can only be adjacent if they have an edge in the graph. The goal is to minimize the number of cells used by colors to maximize the number of 0 cells.

results.content:
FINAL_ANSWER: Use a spanning tree layout with minimal cell occupancy
JUSTIFICATION: Spanning trees minimize cells while maintaining connectivity and adjacency.
